summary: hello244a preinstall exfiltrates /etc/passwd and username to Pipedream endpoint
details: The package's package.json declares a preinstall lifecycle script that runs curl on `npm install` to POST the contents of /etc/passwd and the installer's OS username (interpolated via $(whoami) as a subdomain label) to an attacker-controlled Pipedream request-bin at *.eodxy50gl486xrx.m.pipedream.net. The package has no other content — no main entry, no dependencies, no advertised functionality — its sole effect on install is the exfiltration beacon. Any host that runs `npm install hello244a` will automatically leak its local user database and username to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
